title_left: Umpires, referees, and other sports officials
rationale_left: This job’s core work happens in person, in real time, on the field or court, requiring physical presence, positioning, visual observation, and split-second judgment under pressure. AI may assist at the margins through video replay, training, and administrative tasks, but it is unlikely to replace the core officiating role broadly in the near term.

title_left: Delivery truck drivers and driver/sales workers
rationale_left: This job is centered on physical driving, loading/unloading, and in-person delivery in unpredictable real-world environments, which limits near-term AI automation. But AI can already optimize routing, dispatching, customer communication, and paperwork, and over time advances in autonomous vehicles and delivery systems could significantly reshape staffing and productivity.

rationale_right: While AI and autonomous vehicle technology are advancing, the core of this job involves significant physical labor, such as loading/unloading cargo and navigating unpredictable 'last-mile' environments. AI may optimize routing and assist with sales communication, but the physical requirement to move goods from a vehicle to a customer's door remains a major barrier to full automation.

rationale_left: Air traffic control involves substantial information processing on screens, and AI can assist with routing, conflict detection, weather integration, and decision support—potentially letting each controller manage more traffic. But the core job is real-time, safety-critical control of aircraft in the physical world under heavy regulation, where human judgment, accountability, and rapid coordination remain major barriers to full automation.

rationale_right: The core work is fundamentally digital and data-driven, involving the monitoring of radar screens and the processing of complex spatial information to ensure aircraft separation. While the high-stakes nature of safety and the need for real-time human communication with pilots provide a buffer, AI and automated systems (like NextGen) are already increasing productivity and handling more traffic per controller, leading to a very low projected growth in human employment.

rationale_left: This job includes substantial digital knowledge work—budgeting, reporting, scheduling, compliance monitoring, record management, and analyzing healthcare metrics—where AI can significantly boost productivity. rationale_left: Top executives do substantial digital knowledge work—analyzing reports, reviewing budgets, setting strategy, communicating, and coordinating decisions—so AI can meaningfully augment much of their information processing and planning. But the core of the job also depends on leadership, accountability, negotiation, trust, and high-stakes judgment across real organizations, which limits full automation even as AI may reduce the need for some managerial layers.

rationale_right: Top executives perform high-level knowledge work including financial analysis, strategic planning, and contract review, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the role involves human-centric responsibilities like leadership, high-stakes negotiation, and accountability to boards or the public, which provide a significant buffer against full automation.

But the occupation is not fully automatable because material selection, fit on real bodies, prototype iteration, production oversight, and brand-specific creative judgment still require substantial human involvement.

title_left: Film and video editors and camera operators
rationale_left: AI exposure is very high overall because film and video editing is almost entirely digital, and AI can already assist with organizing footage, rough cuts, color/audio cleanup, effects, and content generation. Exposure is somewhat tempered by camera-operator work, which still requires physical presence, equipment handling, and creative on-set judgment, but robotic cameras and workflow automation will still reduce labor needs and reshape the occupation.

comparison_title_left: Film and video editors and camera operators
title_right: Film and video editors and camera operators
rationale_right: This occupation is a hybrid of physical camera operation and digital video editing. While camera operators have a physical barrier to AI due to the need for on-site equipment management and real-world positioning, editors work entirely in digital environments where AI is rapidly automating tasks like color grading, sound syncing, and even rough-cut generation. The rise of generative video and AI-driven post-production tools significantly increases the productivity of each worker, potentially reducing the total number of editors needed for complex projects.

rationale_right: Financial management is a predominantly digital knowledge-work occupation focused on data analysis, reporting, and forecasting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires significant human judgment, strategic advisory, and leadership, the core technical tasks of monitoring financial details and preparing statements are being rapidly transformed by AI tools.

rationale_left: Historian work is heavily centered on digital and text-based tasks—researching sources, analyzing information, synthesizing findings, translating material, and writing reports or articles—all areas where AI is improving rapidly. rationale_right: Management analysts perform high-level knowledge work—including data analysis, report writing, and financial modeling—that is highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills for client interviews and stakeholder management, the core 'back-office' tasks of synthesizing information and developing efficiency recommendations are increasingly being streamlined by AI tools.

rationale_left: This occupation is highly exposed because much of its core work is digital information processing: researching, analyzing, writing, editing, summarizing, and producing content for multiple platforms. AI can already assist substantially with drafting, background research, transcription, headline generation, and story adaptation, though in-person reporting, source development, interviews, and on-the-ground judgment keep it from being near the maximum.

rationale_right: The core work product is digital (text, scripts, and multimedia), and AI can already automate significant portions of research, drafting, and editing. While physical presence for field reporting and the cultivation of human sources provide a protective barrier, the overall occupation faces high exposure as AI tools drastically increase individual productivity, potentially leading to further industry consolidation and fewer required roles.

rationale_left: Sociologists do predominantly digital knowledge work: designing studies, analyzing data, synthesizing findings, and writing reports and presentations, all of which are areas where AI is advancing quickly. Human judgment remains important for research design, interpreting social context, interviewing people, and policy relevance, but AI can substantially increase productivity and reshape much of the occupation.

rationale_right: Sociology is a knowledge-intensive field where core tasks like data analysis, literature reviews, and report writing are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. While qualitative field research and interpersonal interviews provide a buffer, the shift toward digital data collection and AI-driven statistical modeling significantly increases worker productivity and reshapes the research process.

rationale_left: This job mixes substantial computer-based planning, coordination, records management, policy review, and communication—areas where AI can boost productivity—with important onsite responsibilities like monitoring facilities, supervising maintenance staff, and handling building safety, repairs, and compliance. AI is likely to automate or streamline the administrative and analytical parts of the role, but the physical oversight, vendor coordination, and real-world judgment keep exposure below the highest tier.

rationale_right: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can significantly automate administrative tasks like recordkeeping, supply procurement, and energy pattern analysis, the role requires a physical presence to inspect facilities, oversee manual labor (janitors, repair workers), and manage real-world safety and security compliance.

rationale_right: Lodging managers perform a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can automate back-office tasks like revenue management, dynamic pricing, and staff scheduling, the core role requires physical inspections of facilities and high-stakes interpersonal conflict resolution with guests and staff that cannot be fully digitized.

rationale_left: This occupation has substantial exposure because important parts of the work are digital and information-based: reviewing drawings, using 3D design software, analyzing test data, making calculations, and documenting recommendations. But exposure is capped by the hands-on physical side of the job—assembling parts, operating machinery, setting up tests, troubleshooting equipment, and working in factories or labs—which still requires human presence and manual skill.

rationale_right: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical labor. While AI can significantly automate digital tasks like 3D modeling, data analysis, and cost estimation, the role still requires a substantial physical presence for assembling parts, setting up vehicle crash tests, and operating machinery like lathes and grinders.

rationale_left: Special education teaching includes substantial knowledge work that AI can assist with, such as drafting IEPs, adapting lessons, preparing materials, tracking progress, and handling documentation. But the core job still depends heavily on in-person instruction, behavioral support, individualized judgment, and close human relationships with students, parents, and staff, which limits full automation.

rationale_right: AI will significantly streamline the digital and administrative aspects of the role, such as drafting Individualized Education Programs (IEPs), adapting lesson plans, and tracking student data. However, the core of the job requires intense physical presence, emotional intelligence, and real-time behavioral management for students with complex disabilities, which AI cannot replicate.

rationale_left: This job’s core work is hands-on: inspecting, adjusting, testing, and maintaining physical measurement devices in labs, plants, and other on-site settings. rationale_right: This occupation involves a significant physical component, requiring manual dexterity to adjust hardware and presence in industrial or laboratory environments. While AI can automate data recording, scheduling, and the analysis of calibration deviations, the core tasks of physical inspection, maintenance, and hands-on equipment manipulation provide a natural barrier to full automation.

rationale_left: This job’s core work is physical and site-based: inspecting structures, using testing equipment, climbing through spaces, and verifying real-world conditions against code. AI can help with plan review, report writing, code lookup, scheduling, and some remote inspection workflows, but it cannot replace most onsite inspection duties, so exposure is moderate rather than high.

rationale_right: The occupation is a hybrid of physical site visits and digital knowledge work. AI and computer vision can significantly automate the 'plans examiner' and report-writing functions, while remote inspection technology is already projected to reduce labor demand. However, the core requirement for physical presence to navigate tight spaces, use manual testing equipment, and verify real-world conditions provides a substantial barrier to full automation.

rationale_left: This work is centered on physical fabrication, repair, finishing, and quality inspection of medical devices, dentures, and lenses, which limits direct AI automation because it requires hands-on dexterity, specialized materials handling, and use of tools and machinery. AI and related automation can still materially reshape the occupation through CAD software, 3D printing, and automated lens or dental part production, increasing productivity and reducing labor needs in some specialties.

rationale_left: This job’s core work requires in-person patient care, physical positioning, hands-on operation of ultrasound equipment, and real-time adjustment of the scan based on what is seen, which creates a strong barrier to full AI automation. AI can meaningfully assist with image quality checks, abnormality detection, measurements, documentation, and summaries, but it is more likely to augment sonographers than replace most of the role.

rationale_right: This occupation is a hybrid of physical inspection and data analysis. While AI-powered computer vision systems are rapidly automating the visual detection of defects, the role still requires significant physical presence to handle materials, operate manual gauges, and perform mechanical tests that robots cannot yet fully replicate in diverse manufacturing environments.

title_left: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers
rationale_left: This work is centered on physical presence, patrolling property, controlling access, and responding to incidents in real time, which creates a meaningful barrier to full AI replacement. However, AI-enabled cameras, remote monitoring, and automated alerting can reduce demand for some monitoring and surveillance tasks, especially for gambling surveillance officers and stationary guard roles.

comparison_title_left: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers
title_right: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers
rationale_right: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and digital monitoring. While AI-powered computer vision is rapidly automating the 'surveillance' and 'monitoring' aspects of the job, the core requirements of physical patrolling, detaining suspects, and responding to real-world emergencies provide a significant barrier to full automation.

rationale_left: The core of this job is physical, place-based, and highly interpersonal: escorting groups, managing logistics in real time, and engaging visitors on-site. AI can help with peripheral tasks like research, itinerary planning, translation, marketing, and recordkeeping, but it is unlikely to replace the in-person presence, adaptability, and customer interaction at the center of the occupation.

rationale_right: While AI can automate the digital aspects of the job—such as researching history, planning itineraries, and providing multilingual translations—the core of the role requires physical presence and real-time human interaction. Guides must physically escort groups through unpredictable environments, manage group dynamics, and provide a personal, empathetic experience that digital tools cannot fully replicate.
